articles.title: Kerala man shuts Rs 3 lakh monthly revenue startup, takes Rs 25,000 sales job. Here’s why
articles.description: A Kerala man watched his startup dream collapse and chose a steady salary over another risky venture. That one difficult decision later opened doors to a global business and a real-world MBA.

articles.title: Iran Defies US Warships? Satellite Images Show 'OIL TANKERS LOADING' At Kharg; 'NOTHING CAN STOP...'
articles.description: Fresh satellite imagery is challenging U.S. claims that Iran’s oil system is collapsing under pressure. Images from Kharg Island — the backbone of Tehran’s crude exports — show multiple tankers actively loading oil, signaling that operations are still underway despite the naval blockade. According to Bloomberg, the number of vessels anchored near the island has surged, suggesting Iran has accelerated loading activity since the blockade began. The visuals directly contradict statements by U.S. President Donald Trump, who claimed Iran’s oil network was “choking.”

articles.title: Salman Ali Agha's Revival of IND vs PAK Asia Cup Handshake Controversy Sparks Fan Backlash (WATCH)
articles.description: Pakistan T20I captain Salman Ali Agha has revived the Asia Cup 2025 handshake controversy, recalling Team India’s refusal during their clash amid the Pahalgam terror attack protest. Agha said he was informed of the “no-handshake” policy and felt it was not right for players as role models, reigniting debate on social media among cricket fans.

articles.title: Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas introduces wife Kavya Reddy with caption: 'I found something far greater, World, meet my wife'
articles.description: Tollywood actor Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas has married his longtime partner, Kavya Reddy, in a private ceremony. He introduced his wife to the world with a heartfelt Instagram post, calling her 'fire.' The couple later hosted a grand reception in Hyderabad, attended by numerous prominent stars, who showered them with blessings and good wishes.

articles.title: Why New York mayor Zohran Mamdani kept his distance from King Charles
articles.description: A muted welcome, a colonial past and a pointed remark about the Koh-i-Noor shaped an unusual royal visit.

articles.title: Man held for threatening woman with private photos
articles.description: A Child Welfare Committee member in Ramanagara, Haniyur Chandregowda, faces arrest for allegedly blackmailing a woman with explicit photos and videos, forcing her into a sexual relationship. Police discovered similar compromising material of other women on his phone. He also reportedly defrauded her of lakhs and used her documents fraudulently.

articles.title: 3 DRG jawans injured in IED blast in Chhattisgarh
articles.description: Three District Reserve Guard (DRG) personnel sustained injuries in an improvised explosive device (IED) blast that occurred in Chhattisgarh's Kanker district on Saturday. Security forces have initiated a search operation in the vicinity following the incident. Further details on the condition of the injured and the blast's circumstances are pending.

articles.title: Bargi boat tragedy: Search expanded as four still missing; FIR only after bodies recovered, says cop
articles.description: Rescue teams expand the search radius to 5km for the four people still missing in the Bargi Dam boat accident. Jabalpur police confirm an FIR will be registered only after all bodies are recovered.

articles.title: Monsoon 2026: Here's IMD’s latest prediction on arrival dates for Kerala to Delhi
articles.description: In its first long-range forecast issued in April, the IMD predicted that India is likely to receive below-normal monsoon rainfall during the June-September season. Rainfall is estimated at 92% of the Long Period Average (LPA), with the possibility of El Niño conditions developing later.

articles.title: RBI Governor Malhotra calls for deeper, more efficient financial markets as India shows resilience amid global headwinds
articles.description: Indian financial markets are showing strength amid global challenges. RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra highlighted that future growth hinges on increasing liquidity and participation. The economy is growing fast, with inflation under control. The banking sector is robust. The RBI is implementing measures to improve market efficiency and transparency.

articles.title: How the middle east crisis may affect Kerala election results 2026
articles.description: Kerala's 2026 assembly elections saw a record 78.27% voter turnout, with exit polls indicating a close contest between the UDF and LDF. Attention now turns to May 4th results, with questions arising about the potential impact of the Middle East crisis on the state's deep ties to the Gulf.

articles.title: A day after CEO Tim Cook said Mac products will be hit by chip shortage, Apple stops selling 256GB Mac mini in US, price for 512GB starts at ...
articles.description: Apple has discontinued the 256GB Mac mini, raising the starting price to $799 with the 512GB model now being the baseline. This change coincides with Apple CEO Tim Cook's warning of several months of chip supply constraints, particularly impacting the Mac mini and Mac Studio due to higher-than-expected demand for AI-focused devices.

articles.title: Dhruv Global School Soars in CBSE Results; Ishanvi Barpanda Tops with 99.2 percent
articles.description: Dhruv Global School, Nande, Pune, delivered outstanding results in the CBSE Class 10 examinations 2025–26, with 41 of 63 students scoring above 90 percent. Ishanvi Barpanda topped the school with 99.2 percent, followed by Saish Kedar Bhurke and Yuvika Tripathi with 98.4 percent, and Aryan Jagtap with 98 percent. Thirty students achieved perfect scores across subjects, reflecting strong academic excellence, disciplined learning, and effective guidance from teachers, parents, and school leadership throughout the year respectively.

Her recent posts highlight the benefits of natural cortisol spikes and intentional training, signaling a more thoughtful approach to health. With her upcoming wedding to Derek Green, this phase reflects both personal motivation and a broader effort to connect with followers through purpose-driven fitness content.

articles.title: Iran Plans To Deploy Mine-Carrying Dolphins To Bomb US warships In Strait Of Hormuz
articles.description: A chilling new threat is surfacing in the Strait of Hormuz. Reports suggest Iran may deploy mine-carrying dolphins to strike U.S. warships, turning one of the world’s most vital oil routes into a covert battlefield. With its navy weakened, Tehran appears to be embracing unpredictable, underwater warfare. If true, such tactics could bypass traditional defences, escalate tensions rapidly, and send shockwaves through global markets. In a region already on edge, even the hint of this strategy raises the risk of sudden, devastating confrontation.

articles.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's ‘unpredictable’ nature can put fear, deemed good enough for Tests: ‘If he batted at 5...’

articles.title: Anupamaa actress Madalsa Sharma recalls a famous director asking her to wear a bikini at 19, saying, “I want to see your body language”
articles.description: Actress Madalsa Sharma, known for 'Anupamaa', revealed a disturbing encounter from her teenage years in the film industry. A renowned filmmaker allegedly asked her to wear a bikini privately to assess her comfort level. Sharma firmly stated that while she's open to costumes for on-screen roles, private auditions of that nature were unacceptable, asserting her professional boundaries.

articles.title: Chirayu Rana’s sexual harassment allegations against Lorna Hajdini are ‘scam’: Podcaster Joe Rogan tears into ex-JP Morgan banker
articles.description: Joe Rogan and Shane Gillis dismissed sexual harassment claims made by former JPMorgan employee Chirayu Rana against Executive Director Lorna Hajdini as "fake." Rana accused Hajdini of drugging him, forcing sexual encounters, and threatening his bonus with racist taunts. JPMorgan denies the allegations, stating their internal probe found no evidence of wrongdoing.

articles.description: Commercial LPG: Bengaluru hotels plan 10-15% menu price hike after sharp rise in cylinder costs, with tea, coffee and meals set to get costlier, while migrants and small eateries struggle amid fuel scarcity and rising input expenses.

articles.title: West Bengal polls: Lockdown in strongroom areas after TMC, BJP clash
articles.description: Kolkata police have imposed strict prohibitory orders around EVM strongrooms following Trinamool's vote tampering claims and clashes with BJP workers. Security has been heightened with additional forces and sealed roads. The Election Commission vows to punish any disruption, emphasizing robust security at all counting centers statewide to ensure a peaceful electoral process.

articles.title: Weather Today: No rain in Delhi, yellow alert for 7 Kerala districts. Check IMD's forecast for Bihar, Bengal, Tamil Nadu and other states
articles.description: Weather update: India braces for above-normal May rainfall, with the southwest monsoon expected around May 14-16. Delhi anticipates partly cloudy skies and light rain with thunderstorms on May 3. Kerala faces a yellow alert for heavy showers and strong winds across all districts. Widespread rain has been forecast for various regions, including heavy downpours in the Northeast and East, as per the IMD.

articles.title: Texas nurse who fell into coma on honeymoon in Japan is off life support, breathing on her own; family shares hopeful update
articles.description: A newlywed bride, Sarah Danh, has shown remarkable recovery after a sudden medical emergency in Japan. She was diagnosed with acute liver failure and placed on life support. Now, she is breathing independently and off life support. Her family reports gradual improvement, with Danh responding to loved ones and even taking steps with assistance.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Nicolas Cage: 'The end of the world is on people's minds. We have the power to destroy or...' – inspiring lessons on responsibility, human power and how our choices shape the world by Oscar-winning actor of Leaving Las Vegas
articles.description: Quote of the day by Nicolas Cage: Nicolas Cage emphasizes that humans possess immense power to shape the world, capable of both destruction and salvation. He highlights that our daily choices, not just grand actions, influence global outcomes. The actor's quote urges introspection on how we wield this power and responsibility, stressing conscious action over fear of the future.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Benjamin Franklin: 'Life's tragedy is that we get old...'—Inspiring quotes by the the Founding Father of the United States
articles.description: Benjamin Franklin's poignant quote, "Life's tragedy is that we get old too soon and wise too late," reflects on the human tendency to gain wisdom only after time has passed. The article explores Franklin's life, his multifaceted achievements, and the enduring relevance of his words in today's fast-paced world, urging reflection and valuing time.
